title: KRUPS Precision Coffee and Spice Grinder: 3 oz Capacity, 200W Motor, Stainless Steel Blades for Uniform Grinding - Versatile for Coffee Beans, Spices, Herbs, and Nuts (Black)
description:
About this item
Versatile grinding: 200W motor and stainless steel blades efficiently grind coffee beans, spices, herbs, and nuts. Customize from coarse to fine for various brewing methods: cold brew, French press, drip, pour-over, and espresso.
Large capacity: 3 oz/85g bean hopper grinds enough for up to 12 cups of coffee. Perfect for preparing multiple servings or grinding larger quantities of spices for meal prep. Ideal for households, small offices, or entertaining guests.
One-touch operation: Simple and intuitive design allows for easy grinding with just one button. Press and hold to achieve desired grind consistency, ensuring precise control over your coffee or spice preparation.
Safety first: Lid-activated safety mechanism ensures blades only spin when the lid is securely in place. Provides peace of mind during operation, prevents accidental spills or injuries. Designed with user safety in mind, suitable for all households.
Compact design: Sleek and space-saving construction fits easily on countertops or in cabinets. The black finish complements various kitchen decors, making it a stylish addition to your culinary workspace.
reviews_5s:
Every company wants to claim their product is quiet but this one is reasonably accurate about it. It's a grinder so there's some noise, but it's not jarringly loud at all and is pretty quiet for morning use. The grinder is powerful, fast, and neat, and does a great, even job on the coffee. I'm glad that I went with the model I chose, it's been easy to clean. No complaints, very happy with it so far.Hi everybody!

I bought this particular Krups coffee grinder mainly because my mom has one that is coming up on 20 years old. As the saying goes, "they just don't make em like they used to." Or do they? The answer is YES. Side by side comparison of the 20 year old white Krups, and my brand new black Krups revealed almost no difference. They are identical. You know what that means to me? It means the company knows they make a great product that lasts for years, so why change it? All I can say is I wish Krups made cars.

So, about the grinder... It is very simple and straightforward. A powerful machine with one purpose: to pulverize anything you put in it. I mainly use mine for coffee, but I also use it for spices occasionally. Normally I hand grind all my spices in a mortar and pestle. I have even used it to grind my home grown dry chili peppers to put in a shaker. It works beautifully! The only problem with that is cleaning, but its not fair to fault the machine for that. After all, it was meant for coffee beans. It does take a bit of work to clean it without damaging it. You can't exactly put it in the dishwasher. I used a dry toothbrush and toothpicks to pick out all the spicy mess. I assume I got it all out since I don't taste any chili pepper in my coffee. Heck, for 20 bucks, its worth buying a second one just for spices!

This is a great grinder! It is well built and very powerful! There is no doubt in my mind that this one will last 20+ years just like my moms!

UPDATE 2/2016! It's been about 4 years and this grinder is still going strong! I use it about 2 to 3 times daily. I haven't had one single problem with it, and I have not had to perform any sort of maintenance except for the occasional cleaning. I have noticed a few gripes from other reviewers that I would like to address:

1. Hard to pour the coffee into the coffee maker from the grinder. Really? Come on. Grind your coffee, turn the thing upside down with the lid on, give it a few taps, and pull the lid off in the upside down position. Now you have a nice little container of ground coffee to pour it into your machine. It's not brain surgery... you can do this. Give it a try!

2. Oval shape hinders the circular flow of beans while grinding. I have found this only matters when you overload the grinder. Keep it to level with the top and you're golden. If you're like me and insist on filling the thing with a huge heap, then do this: I solved this by pulsing the power button 5 or 6 times. While doing that, you can watch the grounds circulate top to bottom, not round and round. Either way, your beans still get pulverized, so who cares?

3. Comparing this unit to a high dollar burr grinder. NO! STOP IT! This is a $20 blender type (for lack of a better term) It's not a barrista's wet dream $300 burr grinder. It's just not, no matter how much you want it to be. If that's what you were expecting when you dropped a whole Jackson on this one, you must enjoy disappointment. It's like comparing apples to.. hmm... applesauce.. yeah, applesauce. Lets go with that.

4. Too loud. I saw one reviewer that described this unit as "deafeningly loud" which I had to chuckle at. Granted, it's not exactly quiet, but it's not "deafening" in any sense of the word. If you're one of those spastic drama queens who overly exaggerates everything, this one is not for you. The grinder is basically a drill motor with a tiny lawn mower blade on it. It's gonna make some noise. I'd say it's comparable to a hair dryer on low. I am rather biased on the noise level though. I work in a steel mill, so to me the noise level is no big deal. Take that for whatever it's worth to ya, but it's hardly deafening or I would most certainly be stone deaf by now.

I will update again sometime in the future! ta ta!

10 YEAR UPDATE!!! I have nothing to complain about.. literally nothing! This grinder still works just as good as the day it was new! Considering at least a pot a day sometimes more, we are talking over 3,650 uses. That also makes my moms grinder I mentioned 30 years old and hers is also still going strong. I challenge you to show me any product that lasts 10 years without a single problem. Some may say "Its not the years, its the mileage." which is true to an extent... however, I am not nice to this grinder.. Its not a once-in-a-while thing. Its had daily use since day one. We've been through a lot together and she's got a lottta rough miles on 'er... but the most important thing is she's still here and just won't give up.I have to say that I'm rarely impressed at product quality anymore because I hardly ever see it.

Even some of my favorite brands of just gone down the toilet.

This one though surprised me.

Whoever designed this coffee press must drink coffee because the removable stainless steel container that the coffee beans go in is brilliant! 🤓

One of my pet peeves of coffee grinders is how do you clean out the inside bowl without getting water inside of the mechanical mechanisms? This solves that issue flawlessly.

Well thought out ...

Furthermore, the grinder is absolutely beautiful.

Grinders are usually one color and you might get a modern one but they're just a grinder. However, every time I look at this one, I think it's very elegant and beautifully done.

Again, well thought out.

Another thing I really appreciate is the ability to wrap the cord underneath it and tuck it away in the corner of the kitchen.

Again, well thought out ...

The one drawback, if you can say that, is the lid.

I'm a neat freak and so I want that lid completely dried out before I place it back on the top of the grinder. That's almost impossible to do with a cloth, though, because you just can't get between the two lips on the lid.

Having said that, I found if I lay the lid on sideways on top of the grinder the air will get up In between the lips of the lead and it will air dry eventually so I've come to peace with that.

As for the grinding of the coffee while that's just a whole other level.

For one it is incredibly quiet.

I felt like I went from using a tank to using a fan.

The quietness is unreal. Living in apartment I'm always concerned that people in other apartments can hear what I'm doing and with this grinder I grind anytime I want because I know it's so quiet.

Well thought out ...

The grinding of the beans is amazingly fast and thorough.

I did struggle with that at the beginning as some people on here have stated here but I found as a coffee press user if I do five spins - possibly six - I get the beans just about where I want them.

Perfection isn't my goal - I'm just looking to make sure that my beans aren't too ground but yet that they're ground enough so the beans don't get caught in the mash of the coffee while making a decent cuppa coffee.

I will definitely keep coming back to Krupps and looking at their products because this was so well produced and thought out. I was truly impressed, especially for the price.

To be honest, having lived in Germany, I don't know why I'm surprised because the Germans are really good at what they produce/build.This KRUPS grinder is perfect. I typically make one pour-over cup each day. This is easy, does a nice grind, and no mess. It is pretty quiet, which was not essential to me. It even has a lid to set things aside if you want to let your grind rest. I had the Kitchen Aid version that has a lid you must put on to use, and it would often come off and make a huge mess. I would definitely recommend this greatly over competitors. Excellent coffee and spice grinder. My only wish is that it came with some kind of a brush to get grinds out if they clump. I can just get one from the Hardware store. It would be a nice add.Grinds coffee beans to perfection so they don’t overheat, holds more beans in the feeder than most, and is noticeably more quiet than smaller, cheaper grinders.This may be the best grinder I've owned. It grinds well, and feels well made. I buy whole bean coffee and grind half the bag at a time. It works like a charm. I put coffee in, careful to not overfill. I push down to grind and count the seconds. This way, every time I grind, I consistently have the grind I like. For me, 20 seconds or so, max of 23. I don't wash with water, I just wipe out with a paper towel. I don't grind anything but coffee.My American sister recommended this grinder to me, as originally I wanted to use a Morter and pestle to grind spices to make ranch dressing from scratch. My American mom said the morter and pestle wouldn't be a good idea because it would make a big mess on the kitchen counter. Fast forward to November 3, 2024, when I purchased this grinder, which is one of my favorite colors, silver. I opened it up on Thanksgiving Day, and I tried it out for the first time while we were cooking Thanksgiving dinner. I absolutely looooooooooooooooooooooooooove it a lot! I love the fact that when grinding, the sound is contained within the inside of the machine, and for me, it's basically mess-free. Better yet, whatever I'm grinding won't stick to the sides of the jar. When taking off the jar, though, I have to make sure I'm careful, because it makes the grinds come out the bottom. But I can't complain, and I know this is one amazing grinder! I haven't tried coffee with it yet, as I want to get a second grinding jar for that, as Mom says that coffee would taste kinda strange if there were spices left over in that jar. I also love the fact that this grinder is easy to clean. First time I opened it up, mom said to me, "Please be so careful!". As I am sight challenged, we had to do it together the first time, as she wants me to be safe since the blade is sharp. The speed settings are easy for me to understand, just low and high. I put it on low to grind the spices, as I don't wanna overheat the machine! Grinding parsley, chives, and dillweed didn't take long. Thyme took longer, however, as that particular spice seems a bit coarse compared to the others. I give this grinder a solid 5 stars! How about 1,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000,000 stars!I use this at least twice a day, and have for the past 11 years. EVERY SINGLE DAY. I have never cleaned it, nor had to. Grind coffee beans only. It is well seasoned I guess I could state.

Unbelievable how in this day and age something can be engineered to last like this. I'm not here to buy another, as I will never have to. Just browsing for a new coffee maker and found this in my purchase history. They just don't make anything that lasts like this anymore. Fantastic.

I just find something as durable as this quite unique and extremely rare.We received a whole coffee bean subscription for Christmas and suddenly found ourselves in need of a grinder.
Granted this is my first at home grinder so I dont have a comparison, but its so fast!!! Literal seconds and its blitzed the beans down to a fine aromatic powder.
Its also pretty quiet! My cat who startles at any loud noises didn't react at all. So any pet people in need of a quick cup in the morning should consider this one.
Its a ✨️💪🏻sturdy✨️ feeling machine and seems like it should lastI bought a coffee grinder on Amazon a few months ago and went cheap. I think the price was around $16. It would grind coffee well, but that was end of the good features. It was messy and hard to clean. I finally caved and splurged on this "luxury item" from Krups. What a difference! The Krups is incredibly quiet and very easy to clean. With a removable grinding bowl, you can just dump the ground coffee into your coffee filter without turning the entire coffee grinder upside down. Then, you just rinse the grinding bowl off in the sink. Wow, such a better design.
reviews_1s:
PFAS are forever chemicals that are almost impossible to avoid but if your adding them to your coffee because the grinder you bought is made of PFAS then your mainlining toxic chemicals into your body through your coffee ritual.

I didn’t know Krups uses PFAS in their grinder until I bought this grinder (I had one from a number of years ago that stopped working but Ive been grinding my beans at the grocery store since then).

I contacted Krups through their online customer support re: this specific product using the serial numbers on the bottom. Their reply came late and confirmed PFAS comes into contact w the “water chamber” of what I assume to be another product of theirs, ie probably a coffee maker (not a grinder). OK cool!

Krups not only failed to address the question I had about this grinder but it and Amazon is complicit in directly adding PFAS into kitchen equipment that is coming directly into contact w food. Honestly, this is wild.

Bad bad bad on many levels. Shocked TBH.

Update: when I corrected them (Krups customer support) on the product they asked for a photo of the sticker label warning on the box. “Mngr said PFAS are not coming into contact w the coffee beans” I’m paraphrasing. WTH do I know. Nada. But I’m glad CA is doing something. Lots of chemicals in products CA is warning us on. Kudos to them. Krups on us yeeeshWorked great for the first 6 months. Silent vortex became less silent after 6 month, and after 8 months it started to sound more like a lawn mower. I tested it without removable grinding bowl, and the motor was very quite. Put it back and turned it on without coffee beans, and it was loud again. Called Krups, and they said that I need to send the unit to them for evaluation. I told them that I isolated the problem to the grinding bowl and they agreed to send new one to me. Two weeks later I received UPS from France with the part. Plugged it into the unit, turn it on and the sound was even louder. I sent email to Krups and explained the situation. In response they emailed me UPS label for return of the coffee grinder for evaluation and repair. I suggested that no company in the world can afford repair of $35 appliance and they should send me a new one and I return them old, but they refused. I gave up. Beware.When I bought this Krups One-Touch Coffee and Spice Grinder, I imagined a sleek, futuristic device to transform my beans and spices into powdery perfection. What I got instead was a crash course in disappointment engineering.

Upon its first triumphant debut in my kitchen, I loaded it with fresh coffee beans, pressed the "One-Touch" button, and... poof! It emitted a faint whimper before deciding it had already lived its best life. The beans remained smugly whole, and I was left with a device that made a better paperweight than a grinder.

To its credit, it grinds expectations into dust—just not coffee.

The design is lovely, though. If you’re looking for an expensive way to decorate your counter or perhaps a novel conversation starter (“Hey, want to hear about the time my grinder gave up on life before it even started?”), this is your gadget. But for actual grinding? Unless you’re grinding your hopes and dreams, steer clear.

Krups, you’re on thin ice. Next time, I’ll take my chances with a mortar and pestle.

TL;DR: If you want a grinder that works just once and dies with flair, this is the one for you. Otherwise, save your money for something that doesn’t aspire to be a kitchen tragedy.Once upon a time, the name Krups really stood out. It meant quality and dependability. I loved to have a Krups product, as I knew it would supply years of service, and it would meet exacting standards for my coffee experience.

No longer. This is the second grinder that has quit on me in the last 15 months, same model. The first was after about 2 years, and I had made the mistake of washing the grinding cup, so attributed that to my own poor treatment, even if simply endeavoring to do well.

Here, however, I have absolutely babied this grinder. I have treated it with care and according to all specs, and to have it quite on me after only a little over a year, that's simply unacceptable.

It is a real shame, also, as I love the grinder. It is one of the few that doesn't make me hate myself while it is running in the early morning, with a loud, clanging racket. Sadly, complete silence of a machine that has quit is not the alternative I was looking for.

Product support? Nope. Basically, fix it yourself. Literally, it instructs you to take it apart and lubricate the mechanism within. Seriously? Oh, with machine lubricant, for we all simply have that laying around, right?

Once, I would have sang the praises of this grinder, but now not only would I never recommend it, but would not recommend the Krups brand, either. Sad, to see once heralded leaders in manufacturing fall to lower performance standards than Walmart brands.Purchased 2 krups “silent vortex” grinders which at first use were very quiet but one of the removable grinder cups failed with very little use and now its the loudest noisiest grinder you ever heard, in fact the exact opposite of what i was sold.
Good luck finding any warranty information its conveniently not shown on amazon and maybe if you have a few extra days to tackle “the corporation” on the phone you can talk to someone but by evidence they dont sell these apparently required replaceable grinder cups on amazon they dont want you to get one.
With a huge selection of working coffee grinders id select one that works and doesnt offer fancy tech that fails right away. So sad from a once trusted name, but disposable junk is what we have here.Krups One-Touch Coffee and Spice Grinder Review

I had high hopes for the Krups One-Touch Coffee and Spice Grinder, but unfortunately, it fell short in several crucial areas. While its sleek design and one-touch operation might seem appealing at first glance, the grinder's performance leaves much to be desired.

Underpowered Motor:
The most significant issue with this grinder is its underpowered motor. It struggles with even the simplest of tasks, such as grinding coffee beans or spices. Instead of a smooth and efficient process, you’re met with a grinder that seems to labor through every use. Whether you're trying to grind coffee to a medium-coarse consistency or pulverize spices into a fine powder, this grinder seems ill-equipped for the job.

Frequent Clogging:
Another major drawback is its tendency to clog. The grinder’s design appears to be flawed in a way that causes it to get jammed frequently. This is especially problematic when dealing with spices, which can be quite abrasive. The result is a frustrating experience where you have to frequently stop, disassemble, and clean the grinder just to continue.

Overall Performance:
In summary, the Krups One-Touch Coffee and Spice Grinder does not deliver on its promises. Its lack of power and frequent clogging issues render it a less-than-ideal choice for anyone who values efficiency and reliability in their kitchen appliances. I would recommend considering alternative options that offer better performance and durability.Bought this for its removable bowl which would make it easy to clean. Got this during the Prime Day event and this piece is total garbage. The blade felt a bit loose but I didn't think anything of it. Used it for the first time after 3 months and the first pesto I made in it leaked from the bowl into the grinder stump. The loose blade area allows water/oil/ liquids to seep through the cup and get into the grinder. This is unacceptable. Impossible to return this now - Amazon asked me to contact the manufacturer. I think I will need another lifetime to locate the correct link to report a problem with Krups's product on their website.
I am out of $30 and have a garbage product to dispose off. DO NOT BUYShame on me, for buying this product for the second time and wasting my money.

The first machine I bought in 2019 stopped working with smoke coming out from bottom of the unit. I used it grind small quantity of coffee (once every 3-4 weeks; the machine in total ran for ~ 2 minutes each use, with a 10 second cooling period for every 20 seconds of run time). The machine didn't last for more than 9 months.

I bought another unit from Amazon back in June 2020 and this one stopped working within 6 months, with the same exact issue (smoke from bottom of the unit).

I decided to reach out to KRUPS customer service this time and submitted a warranty ticket with them on 11/13/2020. In line with their product, KRUPS customer service is another example of how to drive away customers from a business. After patiently working with them for about 6 weeks, to get a replacement unit, I had finally given up. I WILL NEVER EVER do business with this company again!

Below is a summary of my interaction with KRUPS customer support - everything is via email and there is no way to talk to a live person (there is a customer service number posted on their website, but calling that number always plays a recorded message stating they are not able to take calls, due to Covid).

1. In response to a ticket I submitted on 11/13/20, I was asked by KRUPS on 11/25/20 to provide the proof of purchase / copy of my invoice, which I sent on the same day (11/25/20).

2. On 11/30/20, KRUPS customer service (Rick) asked me to send an image of the defective unit (with the power cord cut). I was asked to cut the power cord, take a picture of it & send it to them and was told a replacement order for a new grinder will be placed once I send the image. I sent the requested image on the same day (11/30/20).

3. On 12/1/20, I provided my shipping address to Rick, as requested. I also received a confirmation on the same day (12/1/20) that a replacement order for a new grinder was placed and that I will receive the new grinder in about 7 - 14 days.

4. It is 1/4/21 today.... which is more than a month since KRUPS customer service confirmed the replacement order was placed. However, I am yet to get the grinder. I sent several follow-up emails over the last 1 month - unfortunately there isn't a response as of yet.I have an older version of this grinder. It is about 30 years old, I use it every day, and it is probably the oldest appliance that I own, and worked perfectly until recently. Unfortunately, my old KRUPS grinder has stopped working so I replaced it with a new version from KRUPS. However, after unpacking the product and plugging it in, I realized it does not grind coffee beans. The motor turns on when you push down on the cover ( just like my old one) but it does not grind the beans. Very strange, I tried this several times, checked the FAQs on the product website, however no success. I have returned the product to AMAZON as defective.I purchased this grinder on Sept. 22, 2023. And on Sept. 20, 2024, it officially died. It worked well while it functioned. However, the problem is that the threaded screw that holds the blade onto the grinding cup is metal while the hole it goes into on the blade assembly is plastic (which means plastic threads to accommodate the metal screw). This obviously means after so much tension on the blade as it cuts through coffee beans, it's eventually going to fail because the plastic shaft threads aren't as strong as the metal screw. This is hugely disappointing especially given the reputation Krups has (or now "has had") for its products. They should have used metal in the blade shaft assembly to match the metal of the screw, but I'm guessing that costs money so they went for a cheaper option.
The verdict: if you want a 12-month coffee grinder, go ahead and order one. If you want something that lasts, buy something else.
